_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L9.5 'Royal-Grade Bridge' composited keeper and produce the L10 'Royal Champion' final variant.

history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L9_5_to_L10.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.input_strategy: Single-image input (L1_Sword_Base scale anchor dropped to allow new vertical extent for central crest + taller horns).

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L9.5 'Royal-Grade Bridge' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the input image: (a) the STEEL SALLET HELM dome, brim, brim rivets, gold band wrapping the helm at the brim line, closed visor with horizontal eye slit and breathing perforations — the FACE remains FULLY CONCEALED at L10 (no skin / hair / eyes / nose / mustache / beard visible); (b) the existing UPWARD-CURVING STEEL HORNS rising from the helm temples with GOLD POINTED SPIKE CAPS at their tips — at L10 the horns grow TALLER and MORE SHARPLY CURVED per Edit Goal item 1; the gold caps remain at the tips; (c) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS with three articulated horizontal lames at the abdomen, the thicker medial ridge, the L8 tonal scrollwork engraving on the medial ridge, and the L9.5 fine GOLD EDGE PIPING running around the perimeter; (d) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (e) the 3-LAME STEEL PAULDRONS with the L8 gold seam rivets, the L9 gold filigree on the upper face of each pauldron — the new L10 step ADDS a fourth uppermost lame with a sculpted lion's-head motif per Edit Goal item 3; (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES extending into STEEL GAUNTLETS with the L9.5 GOLD KNUCKLE-PLATES; (g) the FLANGED STEEL SWORD BLADE with 6 vertical flanges held in the gauntleted anatomical-RIGHT hand — the new L10 step REPLACES the steel head with a GILT GOLD ORNATE LONGSWORD per Edit Goal item 2; (h) the SWORD LEATHER GRIP with leather grip wrap on upper third and two L6 steel grip-rings at the top and bottom of the leather grip — the new L10 step ADDS gold sword grip end caps per Edit Goal item 2 but the sword grip remains wood; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with the embossed sun-burst boss + gold ring around boss base + GOLD CROSS EMBLEM inlaid on shield face + GOLD RIM BAND with gold rivet heads + four iron cross strap-bands radiating from boss — the new L10 step RAISES + GILDS the cross emblem to be raised relief gold, REPLACES the boss dome with a full gold dome with a ruby gem at center, and THICKENS the rim into a wider gold band with embossed gold studs per Edit Goal item 5; (j) the STEEL SABATONS over warm-brown leather boots; (k) the LONGER 3-LAME STEEL TASSETS with outward flare and L9 gold filigree centered on each plus L9.5 gold edge piping — the new L10 step EXTENDS the tassets longer, FLARES them more outward, edges each lame in gold, AND REMOVES the livery skirt band per Edit Goal item 4; (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on anatomical-RIGHT hip and BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; (m) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the FINAL Royal Champion tier. The unit gains its peak silhouette mightiness: helm extends UPWARD with a tall central gold crest (the silhouette envelope grows vertically), the sword blade transitions from steel to fully gilt ornate gold, the pauldrons gain a fourth sculpted lame with lion's-head motif, the tassets flare longer with gold-edged lames and the LIVERY DISAPPEARS (full plate + gold ornament only), and the shield gains a ruby-gemmed gold boss + raised gilt cross + thickened gold rim. CRITICAL gold rendering note: every gold element MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against cool silver-gray steel. Render gold with: saturated buttery-yellow base, soft top-down white-yellow highlight, deep honey-orange shadow on the underside. Each gold element must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint. (1) TALL CENTRAL GOLD CREST + TALLER HORNS — add a vertical fluted GOLD spike rising from the apex of the helm BETWEEN the two existing horns. The crest is approximately 8 inches tall, fluted (multiple vertical ridges running its length), tapering to a sharp point at the top. The crest is BUTTERY POLISHED GOLD, saturated warm yellow with the same gold language as the rest of the unit's gold accents. The crest's base sits on a small gold mount disc at the helm apex; the crest rises straight up between and slightly forward of the horns. ADDITIONALLY, the existing horns are now TALLER (~8 inches up from ~6 inches at L9) and MORE SHARPLY CURVED (more pronounced upward-and-back sweep). The horn bodies remain cool silver-gray steel; the gold spike caps at their tips remain gold. The helm-plus-crest-plus-horns silhouette now reads visibly TALLER than L9.5 — silhouette mightiness rule active. (2) GILT GOLD ORNATE LONGSWORD — UPGRADE the longsword to a fully ornate royal blade: (i) the cross-guard is now solid BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL with chased filigree (fine-line incised scrollwork in slightly darker gold tones running along the cross-guard's length); the L3/L4 brass quillon caps become sculpted gold LION'S-HEAD finials at each tip (small gold lion faces with stylized mane, eyes, snout, ~1 inch wide); (ii) the wheel pommel is now solid gold, slightly larger and engraved with a stylized motif (e.g. crown or sunburst) raised in relief at the center; (iii) the central blade fuller gains a GOLD-INLAID scrollwork pattern running down its length — fine-line gold inlay against the cool silver-gray steel of the blade, hand-painted curling vine motifs; (iv) the leather grip is now wrapped with BRASS-AND-GOLD WIRE WRAP (alternating brass and gold thin metal wire diagonally wound over the leather), giving the grip a richly textured metallic look; (v) the L6 steel grip-rings become buttery gold collar rings. The blade itself remains cool silver-gray polished steel (only the fuller is gold-inlaid, the rest of the blade reads as steel). BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against cool silver-gray steel, NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base; (ii) soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange.  (3) HEAVY ORNATE 4-LAME PAULDRONS WITH SCULPTED LION'S-HEAD GOLD MOTIF — add a FOURTH uppermost layer to each pauldron, sitting ABOVE the existing L8 small upper disc. The new uppermost lame has a SCULPTED LION'S-HEAD MOTIF rendered in buttery polished GOLD — a small gold-trimmed lion's face emboss centered on the upper face of each pauldron, approximately 2 inches in diameter, with stylized lion features (mane, eyes, snout). The lion-face motif sits on a steel base that extends slightly outward from the existing pauldron, broadening the shoulder silhouette. Each pauldron now reads as 4 stacked layers: the new gold-trimmed lion uppermost lame + the L8 small upper disc + the L4/L5 base disc + the L8 downward bicep lame. The shoulder envelope is visibly BROADER than at L9.5 — silhouette mightiness rule active (peak shoulder width). (4) LONG FLARING TASSETS WITH GOLD LAME EDGES AND LIVERY REMOVED — extend each existing 3-lame tasset DOWNWARD beyond mid-thigh (each tasset now ~12 inches tall, up from ~9 inches at L8/L9). The tassets FLARE outward more strongly at the lower edge — the bottom corners are now visibly wider than the top corners with a clear pronounced flare at the lower edge of each tasset. Each lame's edge is now lined with FINE GOLD PIPING — a thin gold line tracing the top, side, and bottom edges of each lame on each tasset. The 3 lames per tasset remain. ADDITIONALLY, the LIVERY skirt band visible through the front gap between the two tassets at L9 / L9.5 is now FULLY REMOVED — there is NO red+mustard cloth visible at the front of the body at L10. The narrow gap between the two tassets now shows either: (a) the steel inner edges of the tassets touching at the top with the gap narrowing to almost nothing, OR (b) a small visible portion of dark blue trousers in the gap. The character is now full plate + gold ornament with NO cloth livery visible at the torso or hip area. The dark blue trousers remain visible at the calf below the lower edge of the longer tassets. (5) ROYAL SHIELD EMBELLISHMENT WITH RUBY-GEMMED GOLD BOSS + RAISED GILT CROSS + THICKENED GOLD RIM — RAISE and GILD the L9.5 inlaid gold cross emblem on the shield face: the cross is now RAISED RELIEF (stands proud of the shield face by approximately ¼ inch), fully gilded buttery polished gold, four equal arms, each arm now slightly thicker. The cross arms still stop short of the iron cross strap-bands. REPLACE the central boss with a FULL GOLD DOME — the cool silver-gray steel boss dome from L7-L9.5 is now entirely buttery polished gold, slightly larger and more pronounced (~4 inches diameter, up from 3.5 inches). At the apex of the gold dome, add a single SMALL ROUND RUBY GEM — saturated red glass-gem with bright catchlight, approximately ¾ inch diameter, set in the center of the gold boss dome. The sun-burst rays radiating from the boss dome become gold (matching the new gold dome) — they are now raised gold rays, not steel. ADDITIONALLY, THICKEN the L9 gold rim band — the rim is now approximately 1 inch wide (up from ~½ inch at L9), with embossed GOLD STUDS evenly spaced around its circumference (~12-16 raised gold studs replacing the L9 flat gold rivet heads). The four iron cross strap-bands remain dark cool-gray iron AT L10 — they are NOT gilded; the gold accents at L10 are: gold rim band, gold studs, gold cross emblem (raised), gold boss dome, ruby gem. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: the helm dome, brim, brim rivets, gold helm circumference band; the closed visor with eye slit and breathing perforations; the cuirass with its 3 horizontal abdominal lames, thicker medial ridge, tonal scrollwork engraving on the medial ridge, and gold edge piping; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with gold knuckle-plates; the leather grip wrap on the sword; the two steel grip-rings on the sword leather grip; the sword leather grip (sword grip remains wood; sword blade replaced per Edit Goal item 2; gold end caps added); the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the mail coif back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm; the round shield shape (only the rim, boss, cross, and rays change at L10). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. Sword arm extended forward and slightly down, gauntleted hand grips upper sword grip, gold gilt-longsword blade dangling at hip height. Shield arm bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. The new central crest + taller horns extend the silhouette UPWARD — that vertical growth is allowed and intentional at L10. DO NOT crop the crest or horns at the top of the canvas — if needed, the model may rescale the figure slightly to keep all features within the canvas. DO NOT shrink the figure aggressively. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep the steel sword blade — the sword blade MUST be fully gilt buttery gold at L10; DO NOT make the gold sword blade dim, brass-toned, bronze-toned, or yellow-painted — it must read as saturated polished YELLOW GOLD METAL; DO NOT replace the sword leather grip with metal — sword grip remains wood with gold end caps only; DO NOT remove the leather grip wrap or the two steel grip-rings on the sword grip; DO NOT add spikes to the gold longsword blade — flanges remain (no spikes); each flange ends in a small pointed gold finial, NOT a long spike; DO NOT make the central crest a feather plume, peacock plume, ostrich feather, or any non-metal crest — the crest is a SOLID GOLD FLUTED SPIKE; DO NOT make the crest curve to either side — it rises STRAIGHT UP from the helm apex; DO NOT shrink the horns at L10 — they grow TALLER and more sharply curved; DO NOT make the horns gold — only the spike caps at the tips are gold (from L9.5); horn bodies remain steel; DO NOT replace the closed visor with an open helm — face remains FULLY CONCEALED; DO NOT show any skin, hair, eyes (other than the slit), nose, mustache, or beard at L10; DO NOT add a 5th lame to the pauldrons — exactly 4 stacked layers at L10; DO NOT make the lion's-head motif a generic emboss or a simple round disc — it MUST be a recognizable lion face with mane, eyes, and snout; DO NOT make the lion's-head motif silver or steel — the lion-trim is GOLD; DO NOT extend the tassets beyond ~12 inches; DO NOT make the tassets one continuous skirt — they remain TWO SEPARATE plates with a vertical gap at the front; DO NOT keep any red+mustard livery skirt visible at L10 — the livery is FULLY GONE at L10 (no cloth visible at the torso or hip area); DO NOT add new cloth garments at the hip or torso to replace the removed livery; DO NOT change the trouser color (trousers remain dark blue / muted steel-blue, visible at the calf); DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT make the central ruby gem any color other than saturated red — NOT blue, NOT green, NOT purple, NOT clear; DO NOT make the ruby gem larger than ~¾ inch — it is a small gem at the center of the gold dome; DO NOT make the gold boss dome the entire shield face — only the central boss area becomes a gold dome; DO NOT gild the four iron cross strap-bands — they remain dark cool-gray iron at L10; DO NOT make the gold cross emblem flat-inlaid at L10 — it is RAISED RELIEF at L10 (stands proud of the shield face); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing direction (still three-quarter view); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
